title: faramea truncata
intro: Faramea truncata, also known as the "Guayusa" or "Night-Flowering Tree", is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its high levels of caffeine and theobromine, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The leaves of the Faramea truncata plant can be prepared as a tea, which is said to have several health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and boosting energy levels. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion to make a tea, which is consumed hot or cold, depending on the desired effect.
